zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard Baskerville , Baron of Eardisley於1373年出生在英國英格蘭賀福爾易亞迪斯利時，他的父親Sir Richard Baskerville38歲，母親Isabella Hampton31歲。他和Joan Everingham至少有1個兒子。他去世於1394年9月16日在他的家鄉享年21歲，埋葬在英國賀福爾金頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard Baskerville , Baron of Eardisley于1373年出生在英国英格兰賀福爾易亞迪斯利时，他的父亲Sir Richard Baskerville38岁，母亲Isabella Hampton31岁。他和Joan Everingham至少有1个儿子。他去世于1394年9月16日在他的家乡享年21岁，埋葬在英国賀福爾金頓。

nameMeanings.name: Baskerville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Boscherville in Eure, France, named with Old Anglo-Norman French boschet ‘copse, thicket’ (a diminutive of Bois ) + ville ‘settlement, town’.

variants.name: Bois
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcide, Antoine, Donat, Marcel, Ovide, Ovila, Raoul, Serge.

French: topographic name for someone living or working in a wood, from (Old) French bois ‘wood’ (from Late Latin boscum ‘bush, shrub, undergrowth’, of ancient Germanic origin), or a habitational name from (Le) Bois, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is also found in Haiti. Compare Debois and the most frequent French form Dubois . Compare also Boie , Boies , and Boyce .
English: variant of   Boyce   and   Boyes  .

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard Baskerville , Baron of Eardisley was born in 1373, in Eardisley, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Richard Baskerville, was 38 and his mother, Isabella Hampton, was 31. He had at least 1 son with Joan Everingham. He died on 16 September 1394, in his hometown, at the age of 21, and was buried in Kington, Herefordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard Baskerville , Baron of Eardisley est né en 1373, à Eardisley, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Richard Baskerville, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Isabella Hampton, avait 31 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Joan Everingham. Il est décédé le 16 septembre 1394, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 21 ans, et a été enterré à Kington, Herefordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard Baskerville , Baron of Eardisley 1373 in Eardisley, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Richard Baskerville 38 und seine Mutter Isabella Hampton 31. Er hatte mit Joan Everingham mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 16. September 1394 im Alter von 21 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Kington, Herefordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Baskerville , Baron of Eardisley è nato il nel 1373 nel Eardisley, Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir Richard Baskerville, aveva 38 anni e il suo madre, Isabella Hampton, aveva 31 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Joan Everingham. Morì il 16 settembre 1394, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 21 anni, e fu sepolto a Kington, Herefordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Baskerville , Baron of Eardisleyが1373年にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアエアルディスリーで生まれた時，父Sir Richard Baskervilleは38歳，母Isabella Hamptonは31歳であった。彼はJoan Everinghamとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1394年9月16日に故郷で21歳で死去し，イギリスハーエフォードシュアイアキントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Baskerville , Baron of Eardisley(이)가 1373년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 에르디슬리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Richard Baskerville(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabella Hampton(은)는 31세였다. 그는 Joan Everingham(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1394년 9월 16일 그의 고향에서 21세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 허퍼드실르 킨그턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Baskerville , Baron of Eardisley nasceu em 1373, em Eardisley, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Richard Baskerville, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Isabella Hampton, tinha 31 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Joan Everingham. Ele faleceu em 16 de setembro de 1394, em sua cidade natal, com 21 anos, e foi sepultado em Kington, Herefordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard Baskerville , Baron of Eardisley родился в 1373 г. в местности эардисли, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Richard Baskerville было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabella Hampton, 31. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Joan Everingham. Он умер 16 сентября 1394 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 21 год и был похоронен в местности кингтон, хирефордшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Richard Baskerville , Baron of Eardisley nació en 1373, en Eardisley, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Richard Baskerville, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Isabella Hampton, tenía 31 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Joan Everingham. Murió el 16 de septiembre de 1394, en su pueblo, a la edad de 21 años, y fue sepultado en Kington, Herefordshire, Inglaterra.
